author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxi. 19, where Jesus dried up the fig-tree; and on the words, Luke xxiv. 28, where He made a pretence as though He would go further.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Matt. xxi. 19, where Jesus dried up the fig-tree; and on the words, Luke xxiv. 28, where He made a pretence as though He would go further.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The lesson of the Holy Gospel which has just been read, has given us an alarming warning, lest we have leaves only, and have no fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That is, in few words, lest words be present and deeds be wanting.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Very terrible!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who does not fear when in this lesson he sees with the eyes of the heart the withered tree, withered at that word being spoken to it, “Let no fruit grow on thee henceforward for ever”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the fear work amendment, and the amendment bring forth fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For without doubt, the Lord Christ foresaw that a certain tree would deservedly become withered, because it would have leaves, and would have no fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That tree is the synagogue, not that which was called, but that which was reprobate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For out of it also was called the people of God, who in sincerity and truth waited in the Prophets for the salvation of God, Jesus Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And forasmuch as it waited in faith, it was thought worthy to know Him when He was present.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For out of it came the Apostles, out of it came the whole multitude of those who went before the ass of the Lord, and said, “Hosanna to the Son of David, blessed is He that cometh in the Name of the Lord.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There was a great company then of believing Jews, a great company of those who believed in Christ before He shed His Blood for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For it was not in vain that the Lord Himself had come to none “but to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in others, after He was crucified, and was now exalted into heaven, He found the fruit of repentance; and these He did not make to wither, but cultivated them in His field, and watered them with His word.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Of this number were those four thousand Jews who believed, after that the disciples and those who were with them, filled with the Holy Ghost, spake with the tongues of all nations, and in that diversity of tongues announced in a way beforehand, that the Church should be throughout all nations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They believed at that time, and “they were the lost sheep of the house of Israel;” but because “the Son of Man had come to seek and to save that which was lost,” He found these also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they lay hid here and there among thorns, as though wasted and dispersed by the wolves; and because they lay hid among thorns, He did not come to find them, save when torn by the thorns of His Passion; yet come He did, He found, He redeemed them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They had slain, not Him so much, as themselves.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were saved by Him who was slain for them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, as the Apostles spake, they were pricked; they were pricked in conscience, who had pricked Him with the spear; and being pricked they sought for counsel, received it when it was given, repented, found grace, and believing drunk that Blood which in their fury they had shed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they who have remained in this bad and barren race, even unto this day, and shall remain unto the end, were figured in that tree.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: You come to them at this day, and find with them all the writings of the Prophets.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But these are but leaves; Christ is an hungred, and He seeketh for fruit; but findeth no fruit among them, because He doth not find Himself among them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He hath no fruit, who hath not Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And he hath not Christ, who holdeth not to Christ’s unity, who hath not charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so by this chain he hath no fruit who hath not charity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle, “Now the fruit of the Spirit is charity;” so setting forth the praise of this cluster, that is, of this fruit; “The fruit of the Spirit,” he says, “is charity, joy, peace, long-suffering.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not wonder at what follows, when charity leads the way.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly, when the disciples marvelled at the withering of the tree, He set forth to them the value of faith, and said to them, “If ye have faith, and doubt not;” that is, if in all things ye have trust in God; and do not say, “God can do this, this He cannot do;” but rely on the omnipotence of the Almighty; “ye shall not only do this, but also if ye shall say to this mountain, Be thou removed, and be thou cast into the sea, it shall be done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And all things whatsoever ye shall ask in prayer, believing, ye shall receive.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now we read that miracles were wrought by the disciples, yea rather by the Lord through the disciples; for, “without Me,” He says, “ye can do nothing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord could do many things without the disciples, but the disciples nothing without the Lord.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who could make even the disciples themselves, was not certainly assisted by them to make them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We read then of the Apostles’ miracles, but we nowhere read of a tree being withered by them, nor of a mountain removed into the sea.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us enquire therefore where this was done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For the words of the Lord could not be without effect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If ye are thinking of “trees” and “mountains” in their ordinary and familiar sense, it has not been done.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But if ye think of that tree of which He spake, and of that mountain of the Lord of which the Prophet said, “In the last days the mountain of the Lord’s house shall be manifest;” if ye think of it, and understand it thus, it has been done, and done by the Apostles.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The tree is the Jewish nation, but I say again, that part of it which was reprobate, not that which was called; that tree which we have spoken of is the Jewish nation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The mountain, as the prophetic testimony hath taught us, is the Lord Himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The withered tree is the Jewish nation reft of the honour of Christ; the sea is this world with all the nations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now see the Apostles speaking to this tree which was about to be withered away, and casting the mountain into the sea.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the Acts of the Apostles they speak to the Jews who gainsay and resist the word of truth, that is, who have leaves and have no fruit, and they say to them, “It was necessary that the word of God should first have been spoken to you: but seeing ye have put it from you” (for ye use the words of the Prophets, yet do not acknowledge Him whom the Prophets foretold, that is, ye have leaves only), “lo, we turn to the Gentiles.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this also was foretold by the Prophets; “Behold, I have given Thee for a light of the Gentiles, that Thou mayest be My salvation unto the end of the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See then, the tree hath withered away; and Christ hath been removed unto the Gentiles, the mountain into the sea.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For how should not the tree wither away which is planted in that vineyard, of which it was said, “I will command my clouds that they rain no rain upon it”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now that in order to convey this truth the Lord acted prophetically, I mean that, as concerning this tree, it was not His will merely to exhibit a miracle, but that by the miracle He conveyed the intimation of something to come, there are many things which teach and persuade us, yea even against our wills force us to believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In the first place, what fault in the tree was it that it had no fruit, when even if it had no fruit at the proper season, that is, the season of its fruit, it would not assuredly be any fault in the tree; for the tree as being without sense and reason could not be to blame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But to this is added, that as we read it in the narrative of the other Evangelist who expressly mentions this, “it was not the time for that fruit.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For that was the time when the fig-tree shoots forth its tender leaves, which come, we know, before the fruit; and this we prove, because the day of the Lord’s Passion was at hand, and we know at what time He suffered; and if we did not know it, we ought of course to give credit to the Evangelist who says, “The time of figs was not yet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then if it was only a miracle that was to have been set forth, and not something to be prophetically figured, it would have been much more worthy of the clemency and mercy of the Lord, to have made green again any tree He might find withered; as He healed the sick, as He cleansed the lepers, as He raised the dead.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But then contrariwise, as though against the ordinary rule of His clemency, He found a green tree, not yet bearing fruit out of its proper season, but still not refusing the hope of fruit to its dresser, and He withered it away; as though He would say to us, “I have no delight in the withering away of this tree, but thus I would convey to you, that I have not designed to do this without any cause for it, but only because I desired thereby to convey to you a lesson you might the more regard.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is not this tree that I have cursed, it is not on a tree without sense that I have inflicted punishment, but I have made thee fear, whosoever thou art that dost consider the matter, that thou mightest not despise Christ when He is an hungered, that thou mightest love rather to be enriched with fruit, than to be overshadowed by leaves.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This one thing is that which the Lord intimates that He designed to signify by what He did.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What else is there?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He cometh to the tree being hungry, and seeketh fruit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Did He not know that it was not the time for it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What the cultivator of the tree knew, did not its Creator know?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He seeketh on the tree then for fruit which it had not yet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Doth He really seek for it, or rather make a pretence of seeking it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if He really sought it, He was mistaken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But this be far from Him, to be mistaken!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He made then a pretence of seeking it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Fearing to allow this, that he maketh a pretence, thou dost confess that He was mistaken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Again, thou dost turn away from the idea of His being mistaken, and so run into that of His making a pretence.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We are parched up between the two.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we are parched, let us beg for rain, that we may grow green, lest in saying anything unworthy of the Lord, we rather wither away.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Evangelist indeed says, “He came to the tree, and found no fruit on it.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “He found none,” would not be said of Him, unless He had either really sought for it, or made a pretence of seeking, though He knew that there was none there.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Wherefore we do not hesitate, let us by no means say that Christ was mistaken.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then? shall we say He made a pretence?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Shall we say this?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How shall we get out of this difficulty?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us say what, if the Evangelist had not said of the Lord in another place, we should not of ourselves dare to say.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let us say what the Evangelist has written, and when we have said, let us understand it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But in order that we may understand it, let us first believe.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “unless ye believe,” says the Prophet, “ye shall not understand.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord Christ after His Resurrection, was walking in the way with two of His disciples, by whom He was not yet recognised, and with whom He joined company as a third traveller.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They came to the place whither they were going, and the Evangelist says, “But He made a pretence as though He would have gone further.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But they kept Him, saying, in the spirit of a courteous kindness, that it was already drawing toward evening, and praying Him to tarry there with them; being received and entertained by them, He breaketh Bread, and is known of them in blessing and breaking of the Bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then, let us not now fear to say, that He made a pretence of seeking, if He made a pretence of going further.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But here there arises another question.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yesterday I insisted at some length on the truth which is in the Apostles; how then do we find any “pretence” in the Lord Himself?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore, Brethren, I must tell you, and teach you according to my poor abilities, which the Lord giveth me for your benefit, and must convey to you what ye may hold as a rule in the interpretation of all Scripture.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Everything that is said or done is to be understood either in its literal signification, or else it signifies something figuratively; or at least contains both of these at once, both its own literal interpretation, and a figurative signification also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thus I have set forth three things, examples of them must now be given; and from whence, but from the Holy Scriptures?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is said in its literal acceptation, that the Lord suffered, that He rose again, and ascended into heaven; that we shall rise again at the end of the world, that we shall reign with Him for ever, if we do not despise Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Take all this as spoken literally, and look not out for figures; as it is expressed, so it really is.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And so also with divers actions.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostle went up to Jerusalem to see Peter, the Apostle actually did this, it actually took place, it was an action peculiar to himself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is a fact which he tells you; a simple fact according to its literal meaning.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The stone which the builders refused, is become the Head of the corner,” is spoken in a figure.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we take “the stone” literally, what “stone did the builders refuse, which became the Head of the corner”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we take “the stone” literally, of what corner is this “stone” become the Head?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we admit that it was figuratively expressed, and take it figuratively, the Corner-stone is Christ: the head of the corner, is the Head of the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Why is the Church the Corner?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because she has called the Jews from one side, and the Gentiles from another, and these two walls as it were coming from different quarters, and meeting together in one, she has bound together by the grace of her peace.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For, “He is our peace, who hath made both one.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard instances of a literal expression, and a literal action, and of a figurative expression; ye are waiting for an instance of a figurative action.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There are many such, but meanwhile, as is suggested by this mention of the corner-stone, when Jacob anointed the stone which he had placed at his head as he slept, and in his sleep saw a mysterious dream, ladders rising from the earth to heaven, and Angels ascending and descending, and the Lord standing upon the ladder, he understood what it was designed to figure, and took the stone for a figure of Christ, to prove to us thereby that he was no stranger to the understanding of that vision and revelation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Do not wonder then that he anointed it, for Christ received His Name from “the anointing.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now this Jacob was said in the Scripture to be “a man without guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this Jacob ye know was called Israel.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Accordingly in the Gospel, when the Lord saw Nathanael, He said, “Behold an Israelite indeed, in whom is no guile.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And that Israelite not yet knowing who it was that talked with him, answered, “Whence knewest Thou me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said to him, “When thou wast under the fig-tree I saw thee;” as though he would say, When thou wast in the shadow of sin, I predestinated thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And Nathanael, because he remembered that he had been under the fig-tree, where the Lord was not, acknowledged His Divinity, and answered, “Thou art the Son of God, Thou art the King of Israel.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He who had been under the fig-tree was not made a withered fig-tree; he acknowledged Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And the Lord said unto him, “Because I said, When thou wast under the fig-tree I saw thee, believest thou? thou shall see greater things than these.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What are these “greater things”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Verily I say unto you” (for he “is an Israelite in whom is no guile;” remember Jacob in whom was no guile; and recollect of what he is speaking, the stone at his head, the vision in his sleep, the ladder from earth to heaven, the Angels ascending and descending; and so see what it is that the Lord would say to “the Israelite without guile”); “Verily I say unto you, Ye shall see heaven opened” (hear, thou guileless Nathanael, what guileless Jacob saw); “ye shall see heaven opened, and Angels ascending and descending” (unto whom?) “unto the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Therefore was He, as the Son of Man, anointed on the head; for “the head of the woman is the man, and the Head of the man is Christ.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now observe, He did not say, “ascending from the Son of Man, and descending to the Son of Man,” as if He were only above; but “ascending and descending unto the Son of Man.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Son of Man crying out from above, “Saul, Saul.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Son of Man from below, “Why persecutest thou Me?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Ye have heard an instance of a literal expression, as “that we shall rise again;” of a literal action, as that, according as it is said, “Paul went up to Jerusalem to see Peter.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The stone which the builders refused,” is a figurative expression; “the anointed stone” which was at Jacob’s head, is a figurative action.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: There is now due to your expectation an example made out of both together, something which is at once a literal fact, and which also signifies something else figured by it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “We know that Abraham had two sons, the one by a bondmaid, the other by a free-woman;” this was literally a fact, not only a story, but a fact; are ye looking for that which was figured in it?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “These are the two Testaments.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That then which is spoken figuratively, is a sort of fiction.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But since it has some real event represented by it, and the very figure itself has its ground of truth, it escapes all imputation of falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “The sower went out to sow his seed; and as he sowed, some fell by the way side, some fell upon stony places, some fell among thorns, and some fell upon good ground.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who went out “to sow,” or when went he out, or upon what “thorns,” or “stones” or “way side” or in what field did he sow?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If we receive this as a fictitious story, we understand it in a figurative sense; it is fictitious.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if any sower really went out, and did cast the seed in these different places, as we have heard, it were no fiction, and so no falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But now though it be a fiction, yet it is no falsehood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Because the fiction has some further signification, it deceives thee not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It requires only one to understand it, and does not lead any one into error.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And thus Christ wishing to convey this lesson to us, sought for fruit, and hereby set forth to us a figurative, and no deceiving fiction; a fiction therefore worthy of praise, not of blame; not one by the examination of which we might run into what was false; but by the diligent investigation of which we might discover what is true.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I see that one may say, Explain to me; what did that signify, that “He made a pretence of going further”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For if it had no further meaning, it is a deceit, a lie.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We must then according to our rules of exposition, and distinctions, tell you what this “pretence of going further,” signified; “He made a pretence of going further,” and is kept back from going further.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In so far then as the Lord Christ being as they supposed absent in respect of His Bodily presence, was thought to be really absent, He will as it were “go further.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But hold Him fast by faith, hold Him fast at the breaking of Bread.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What shall I say more?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Have ye recognised Him?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If so, then have ye found Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I must not speak any longer on this Sacrament.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They who put off the knowledge of this Sacrament, Christ goeth further from them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let them then hold It fast, let them not let Him go; let them invite Him to their home, and so they are invited to heaven.
